# yandex_cloudregistry_lifecycle_policy (Resource)

A LifecyclePolicy resource.



<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `registry_id` (String) ID of the registry that the lifecycle policy belongs to.

### Optional

- `description` (String) Description of the lifecycle policy. 0-1024 characters long.
- `id` (String) ID of the lifecycle policy to return.
- `name` (String) Name of the lifecycle policy.
- `policy_id` (String) ID of the lifecycle policy to return.
- `rules` (Attributes List) List of lifecycle rules. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules))
- `state` (String) Current state of the lifecycle policy.
- `timeouts` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--timeouts))

### Read-Only

- `created_at` (String) Output only. Creation timestamp.
- `created_by` (String) Output only. ID of the user who created the lifecycle policy.
- `modified_at` (String) Output only. Modification timestamp.
- `modified_by` (String) Output only. ID of the user who last modified the lifecycle policy.

<a id="nestedatt--rules"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules`

Optional:

- `delete` (Attributes) Rule that deletes artifacts. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--delete))
- `docker_filters` (Attributes) Docker-specific filters. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--docker_filters))
- `keep_by_age` (Attributes) Rule that keeps artifacts by age. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--keep_by_age))
- `keep_by_version` (Attributes) Rule that keeps artifacts by version count. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--keep_by_version))
- `maven_filters` (Attributes) Maven-specific filters. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--maven_filters))
- `path_prefix` (String) Path prefix to which the rule applies.
- `version_regexp` (String) Regular expression pattern to match package version or docker tag.

<a id="nestedatt--rules--delete"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules.delete`

Optional:

- `always` (Boolean) Always delete (use with caution).
- `cooldown_period_days` (Number) Cooldown period in days before deletion.
- `older_than_days` (Number) Delete artifacts older than specified days.
- `type` (String) Type of deletion.
- `version_condition` (Attributes) Delete artifacts by version count condition. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--delete--version_condition))

<a id="nestedatt--rules--delete--version_condition"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules.delete.version_condition`

Optional:

- `versions_count_greater_than` (Number) Delete when version count exceeds this number.



<a id="nestedatt--rules--docker_filters"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules.docker_filters`

Optional:

- `tag_status` (String) Filter by tag status.


<a id="nestedatt--rules--keep_by_age"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules.keep_by_age`

Optional:

- `younger_than_days` (Number) Keep artifacts younger than this number of days.


<a id="nestedatt--rules--keep_by_version"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules.keep_by_version`

Optional:

- `keep_versions_count` (Number) Number of versions to keep.


<a id="nestedatt--rules--maven_filters"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules.maven_filters`

Optional:

- `version_type` (String) Filter by version type.



<a id="nestedatt--timeouts"></a>
### Nested Schema for `timeouts`

Optional:

- `create` (String) A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours).
- `delete` (String) A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours). Setting a timeout for a Delete operation is only applicable if changes are saved into state before the destroy operation occurs.
- `read` (String) A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours). Read operations occur during any refresh or planning operation when refresh is enabled.
- `update` (String) A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours).
